rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF JASON KENNETH CHEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/23. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 10/27/23 indicates that notice was mailed on 9/18/23, but the amendment that required notice was not filed until 9/19/23. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) The face of the notice also does not indicate that an AMENDMENT TO PETITION FILED MARCH 10, 2023 was filed. (As noted previously, there is no separate petition for order re deposit of money...) (3) The only pleadings that require notice are the "Petition for Acceptance and Approval of Second Account, Report of Guardian" and "Amendment to Petition Filed March 10, 2023". Those are the pleadings that must be listed in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing. Deadline for addressing issues is 12/1/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERMA L. ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Reducing Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) (3) The value of property on hand, without an accounting, cannot be confirmed at this time. The value of the estate at the end of the last accounting has been verified and could be the basis for reduction in bond. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YOUNG JOON CHO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the last hearing: 1) The probate referee's commission ($1,771) must be paid before the Court can approve final distribution. Please file a copy of a receipt or other proof of payment. 2) An allegation must be made that the petitioner or other beneficiary has advanced sufficient cash (including a refund from the current attorney) to the estate for payment of the attorneys' fees and costs, or the estate real property must be distributed in kind, subject to a lien securing the attorneys' fees and costs. See Probate Code section 10831(d) and LRSF 14.56. Each attorney's "retainer" ($1,500) can only be used to pay costs (but not fees) without prior court approval. The current attorney must refund the retainer balance ($574.32 after deducting $925.68 costs) to the petitioner so that payment of the attorneys' fees and costs can be ordered to be made out of the estate after the Court approves the attorneys' fees and final distribution. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Legal: The Court will need to determine whether sufficient evidence has been presented to overcome the presumption of revocation. Procedural: 1. Neither the proof of service filed on 7/31/23 nor the proof of publication filed on 8/10/23 shows that the will is LOST. Earlier notices (mailed and published) showed that the will is LOST, but notice of the petition, as amended, no longer shows that this is a hearing to admit a LOST will, which could be misleading (as though the will had been found). Does the Court wish to waive defective notice? 2. A copy of the lost will does not appear to have been lodged. Only the earlier, original statutory will is in the will file. 3. No proposed order has been received by this examiner or lodged at the time of this review. A copy of the lost will, if admitted, must be attached to the order. [LRSF 14.20.] Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN PANARELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been addressed since the initial hearing on this petition (03/20/2023): 1) A certified copy of a spousal property order or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of David Lopez' death certificate must be filed to determine Mary Cooley's entitlement to distribution. 2) If Frank Lopez Jr. survived the decedent, a certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative of his estate must be filed, or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of his death certificate must be filed to determine Terrance Lopez/Kevin Lopez' entitlement to distribution. 3) A copy of Denise Lopez' birth certificate or other proof of her child-parent relationship with Frank Lopez must be filed. The only information provided by the supplemental papers was her address. A review of the supplemental papers indicate that the following additional issues must also be addressed: 1) No DE-300 form was attached to the small estate affidavit filed by the successors of Cordelia Womack. The administrator cannot supplement to provide the missing form. The successors must sign an affidavit (or a declaration) with the missing form attached. 2) No petition has been filed under Probate Code section 200 or 12400 to establish the fact of death of Charles Steven Madrid if he is presumed to be dead. 3) Re the DE-120 form filed on 11/03/2023: i) Notice to Ruth Guffey, Emelia Madrid, Charles Corbera, Maria Rojas, Brandon Sandoval, and Jeanie Sandoval was given on 11/03/2023, less than 15 days before this hearing. ii) Notice was not given to the "attorney" for Kemp & Associates. iii) Zachary Madrid's address is different from his address previously verified. 4) Kevin Lopez, identified as a successor of one of the postdeceased heirs, cannot be located to give notice. The Court is to determine whether to take this petition under submission, without determining the shares of Charles Madrid and Michael Madrid Sr. and successors of the postdeceased heirs. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

If the balance exceeds $250,000, a proof must be filed to show that the uninsured portion of the estate cash has been transferred to different institutions in observance of the FDIC limit or has otherwise been invested as authorized by sections 9730 and 10533(a)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIAS S. WEINTRAUB
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The prior tentative ruling specifically directed that an AMENDMENT be filed. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions/distinctions.] (2) An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, which means use of the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 Notice of Hearing with a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice. Alternatively, notice of the "supplements" that amended the petition could be waived by the other two heirs. (3) Page 3, line 5-1/2, of the "second supplemental declaration" filed on 10/25/23 shows a sum that does not appear to be correct Deadline for responding to these issues is 11/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Until an order is actually signed, it is a proposed order. A revised proposed order is still just a proposed order and does not need to be (should not be) captioned as a "revised" order when no order has been entered yet. The purpose of a petition, supplement, or amendment is to disclose information to the interested parties, not just to the court)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEFFREY ALAN MEYERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Procedural issues on Petition: 1) Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to be mailed to Amy Harrington who filed Request for Special Notice. Prob. Code sections 12201(b)1220(a)(2)(B). 2) Reimbursement of funeral expenses paid to the administrators without filing Creditor's Claim. Prob. Code sections 9000(a)(3), 9100, 9252. 3) Reimbursement of travel and legal expenses to the administrators made before satisfying all creditor's claims. 4) A report of status of administration under sec. 12200 is before the court. Additional requests should be filed by a separate petition with a proper filing fee. 5) Proposed Order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. Procedural issue on Objection: 1) A report of status of administration under sec. 12200 is before the court. Petition initially prayed for additional time. Objector may need to file a separate petition with a proper filing fee for any additional relief. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The successor-in-interest to a post-deceased heir is not necessarily the deceased heir's issue. If the post-deceased heir left a will or had a trust, the successor-in-interest would be the executor of the deceased heir's will (if appointed by a court) or the successor trustee of the deceased heir's trust. (2) The proof of service filed on 10/23/23 was incompletely verified (not dated). (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] The proof of service filed on 10/23/23 did not have a copy of those instructions attached to the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. (4) Bond will be required unless all interested parties waive bond. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues by 12/1/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please do not "answer" issues posted in the tentative ruling. A supplement or amendment to a petition must stand on its own and not "respond" to issues like a test answer sheet.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHI ANN COCHRANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/04/2023, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative ruling for 10/23/2023 - also e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/10/2023): 1) No proposed order (DE-226 form) has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Item 4c of the petition: At least one box must be checked. 4) Item 7: i) Attachment 7 must be filed to allege facts in support of property characterization. For community property, allegations required by LRSF 14.26(A) must be included. ii) Attachment 7a must be filed. The property determined to be passing to the petitioner must be described. Property characterization (decedent's separate property, her ½ share of community property, or both) must also be alleged. 5) Item 9: i) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. ii) Is Candy Hirsch the only sibling the decedent ever had? 6) If the vehicle is the only property subject to this petition, please explain why you are not proceeding with the affidavit procedure under Vehicle Code section 5910 (affidavit for transfer without probate).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM EDWARD FOBERT 2015 REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED MAY 5, 2015.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate Code For Order Confirming Trust Ownership Of And Conveyance Of Title Of Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/2024 (the next available date). The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior: 1) Petition to set forth all persons entitled to notice. Prob. Code sec. 851(b). Petition identifies the trust beneficiaries only; however, notice to ascertainable heirs is required. According to the settlor's will, he might have been survived by multiple relatives. 2) Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to be given to all persons entitled, including the persons identified in the above #1. 3) Need authority under which the court can confirm the IRA accounts are the trust asset when this type of account is to be held by an individual, not a legal entity (a trust). 4) Need clarification of the relief sought. If petition seeks a third party to convey the assets to the trust, a proper notice should be given pursuant to Prob. Code sec.851(a). Petition does not pray for conveyance; however, the caption sets forth so. Notice is also silent about conveyance. CRC 7.50 and 7.102. 5) Proposed order includes the conveyance provision in #9 even though the petition is not specifically prayed for. Either Petition or (Proposed) Order to be amended properly. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
